executiveSummary.text: STERIS plc (STE) trades at $222.58 with moderate upside scenarios and limited downside on the downside; the base case assumes stable earnings in a normalised macroeconomic environment. Recommendation: REDUCE at $222.58.

businessModel.text:
STERIS operates in the unglamorous but essential business of keeping medical instruments and pharmaceutical products sterile. Its core product range spans three main categories: sterilisation equipment (steam autoclaves, ethylene oxide EtO processors, and radiation-based systems used by pharma manufacturers), cleaning and disinfection products (instrument washer-disinfectors, enzymatic and alkaline cleaning chemistries), and surgical infrastructure (operating theatre lights, tables, and equipment booms). The company also sells the consumables and spare parts that keep all of this equipment running — sterile barrier products, biological and chemical indicators, and service parts. This breadth gives STERIS a foothold in virtually every setting where infection control matters, from a dental practice in Ohio to a biopharmaceutical production line in Germany.

Customers break into two broadly different cohorts with distinct demand dynamics. The first is the acute care segment — hospitals and ambulatory surgical centres — which buy capital equipment cyclically when budgets allow and then spend on service contracts, parts, and consumables as long as that equipment stays in service. The second is pharmaceutical manufacturers, who use STERIS sterilisers and cleaning systems as part of their production process for drugs and medical devices; this segment is less cyclical but more demanding on validation and regulatory compliance. Smaller channels include dental practices, veterinary clinics, and labs. No single customer accounts for a material share of revenue, which limits concentration risk but also means STERIS is exposed to broad hospital budget sentiment across its core markets.

The aftermarket is the structural advantage of this business model. Once a hospital or surgical centre has installed STERIS sterilisation or surgical equipment, switching is costly and disruptive — equipment must be retrained, recertified, and the changeover itself poses infection control risks. This creates a sticky installed base that generates recurring revenue from multi-year service agreements, annual calibration and certification, consumables repurchase, and periodic mid-life upgrades. STERIS management has consistently highlighted that aftermarket revenue carries higher margins than original equipment — service contracts especially are high-quality, recurring income. The balance between equipment sales and aftermarket mix is therefore a key driver of reported margins, and it shifts with the capital budget cycle.

That capital budget cycle is the central risk. When hospitals are in expansion mode — as they were during the post-COVID capex surge and the building-related enthusiasm of 2023-2024 — equipment orders spike and STERIS re-rates. But hospital budgets are under structural pressure from labour shortages, inflation in clinical consumables, and tighter government reimbursement in both the US and European public systems. At $222.58, near the top of the $209.98–$269.44 range, the stock is pricing in a healthcare capex recovery that has not yet materialised in hard orders. The risk is a margin mix shift: lower-margin equipment sales growth slowing while the high-margin aftermarket base grows only incrementally. REDUCE reflects the view that the upside to the top of the range is limited and the downside risk to the bottom is underpriced.

recentCatalysts.text: No major RNS filings in the trailing three to six months. Market-moving news has been limited; any re-rating will likely depend on macro conditions, sector rotation, or company-specific operational milestones.

thesisEvaluation.text:
ScenarioWeightThesis
              Bull30%Strong demand, margin expansion and multiple re-rating drive meaningful upside. Target: +30-40% from current levels.
              Base50%Steady earnings growth in line with long-term averages. Target: +10-15% over 12 months.
              Bear20%Margin compression, macro headwinds or sector rotation create downside risk. Target: -15-20% from current levels.

risks.risk: Macroeconomic sensitivity — Sector earnings are correlated with global growth, interest rates, and input cost inflation.
Currency risk — GBP/EUR/USD exposure on reported revenues and costs.
Competitive dynamics — Pricing pressure from peers or new entrants.
Liquidity and spread risk — As a non-primary-listing Irish stock, bid/offer spreads may be wide.
Regulatory and political risk — Trade policy, FDI screening, and ESG regulation affecting sector.

recommendation.text:
REDUCE — Conviction: 50/100. Enter on pullbacks toward the 52-week low for better risk/reward. Reduce or exit on strength exceeding the 52-week high on volume. No explicit price target is issued; investors should size positions commensurate with conviction level and portfolio risk budget.
            Entry framework: Accumulate on weakness below the 52-week low or on break above the 52-week high on elevated volume.

entryExit.text:
STERIS plc (STE) — Entry / Exit Framework

Reduce / Trim: Above $227.03 — the stock is trading near or above the 52-week high. Capital should be rotated elsewhere.
Tighten or exit on any bounce above $244.84 — the risk/reward is poor at current levels.
Stop-loss (for remaining exposure): $274.83 — a close above the 52-week high would confirm the bull case is breaking down.
Target: $226.78 — partial mean reversion toward the middle of the 52-week range.

sources.marketData: Live quote data sourced via DYOR HQ data pipeline (Google Sheets + Yahoo Finance). Fields include price, market capitalisation, 52-week range, P/E, EPS, and volume.
